Platformn8n MCP

n8n MCP Integration

Connect your AI agents to n8n's visual workflow builder via the Model Context Protocol (MCP). Agents can trigger any n8n workflow on demand — enabling complex automations across 300+ apps without leaving the chat.

Architecture Flow
AI Agent
OpenClaw
MCP Request
workflow trigger
n8n Workflow
automation
300+ Apps
CRM, email, social...

Two Ways to Connect

Choose the option that fits your setup. Both routes give your AI agent full control over n8n workflows.

A
MCP Server (Recommended)
Install the @n8n/mcp npm package to expose n8n workflows as MCP tools. This gives OpenClaw native, structured access to all your workflows — no webhooks needed.
npm install -g @n8n/mcp
Then register in OpenClaw: openclaw mcp add n8n --command n8n-mcp
B
Webhook Trigger
If you prefer not to install MCP, use n8n's Webhook node as the bridge. Your AI agent calls the webhook URL to trigger any workflow. Works with any n8n instance — cloud or self-hosted.
POST https://your-n8n.com/webhook/your-workflow-id
The AI agent sends a POST request with JSON payload. n8n receives it and executes the workflow.

Step-by-Step Setup

  1. Open n8n and go to Settings → Nodes → HTTP Request. Make sure the node is enabled.
  2. Enable the Webhook trigger node in your workflow (comes pre-installed in n8n).
  3. Ensure your n8n instance is accessible from OpenClaw's server. For cloud n8n: webhooks work out of the box. For self-hosted: ensure the server is reachable via the configured URL.
  1. In n8n, create a new workflow or open an existing one.
  2. Add a Webhook node as the first node (trigger). Set the HTTP method to POST and note the webhook path (e.g., /new-contact).
  3. Add your automation steps — e.g., an HTTP Request to an external API, a Google Sheets node to write data, an email node to send a message.
  4. Connect the nodes and save the workflow.
  5. Copy the Webhook URL shown in the webhook node panel. It looks like: https://your-n8n.com/webhook/new-contact
  1. Option A — MCP Server (recommended):
    openclaw mcp add n8n --command n8n-mcp --args "--url https://app.clawops.studio/n8n"
    Or add to ~/.config/openclaw/settings.json:
    { &quot;mcpServers&quot;: { &quot;n8n&quot;: { &quot;command&quot;: &quot;n8n-mcp&quot;, &quot;args&quot;: [&quot;--url&quot;, &quot;https://app.clawops.studio/n8n&quot;] } } }
  2. Option B — Webhook URL in agent config:Add the webhook URL to your AI agent's task configuration in the ClawOps dashboard under Settings → MCP → n8n.
  3. Restart the OpenClaw gateway to pick up the new MCP configuration:
    openclaw gateway restart

Example Workflows

Popular workflows to trigger from your AI agent.

Example: New Contact → CRM + Email
1
Webhook receives contact data (name, email, phone)
2
HTTP Request creates contact in GoHighLevel
3
Email node sends a welcome sequence email
4
Slack node notifies the sales team
Example: Social Post → Create Content + Schedule
1
Webhook receives post content and target platform (Twitter/LinkedIn)
2
AI agent generates post copy (via OpenAI)
3
LinkedIn node posts the content
4
Notion node logs the post to a content calendar
Example: Lead Qualification → Score + Route
1
Webhook receives lead data from website form
2
Code node calculates lead score based on rules
3
If score > 80: HTTP Request adds to High-Ticket pipeline in GHL
4
If score < 80: adds to Nurture pipeline and schedules follow-up email
n8n's free tier includes 100 workflow executions/month on the cloud plan. Your OpenClaw VPS can run a self-hosted n8n instance with unlimited executions — that's what ClawOps Studio uses.
Webhook security: n8n webhook URLs are public by default. Use the"Response: Node.js" authentication option or add a shared secret header to prevent unauthorized calls.
Error handling: In n8n, add an Error Trigger workflow to catch failures from your main workflows. Connect it to a Slack or email node so you're alerted immediately when an automation breaks.
FAQ
Does n8n have a native MCP server?
n8n does not ship a native MCP server, but the @n8n/mcp npm package (community-built) provides one. It exposes your workflows as MCP tools that OpenClaw can call directly. Alternatively, webhook triggers work for any n8n setup without installing anything extra.
What is the difference between the MCP approach and webhooks?
MCP gives your AI agent structured, tool-like access to n8n — the agent sees available workflows as callable functions and can pass typed parameters. Webhooks are simpler: the agent sends an HTTP POST to a URL and gets back a response. Both work; MCP is more robust for complex use cases.
Can I trigger n8n workflows on a schedule?
Yes. Use n8n's Schedule Trigger node (instead of Webhook) to run workflows on cron. You can also combine both — a Schedule node runs nightly, and a Webhook node lets the AI agent trigger it on demand.
How do I pass data from my AI agent to n8n?
The AI agent formats a JSON payload (matching your webhook's expected body) and sends it via POST. In n8n, the Webhook node's body is available as an object in subsequent nodes — access fields with expressions like {{ $json.email }}.
My webhook URL is not reachable. What do I check?
1) Make sure n8n is running and the webhook is active (workflow must be turned on). 2) For self-hosted: check the webhook URL in n8n settings matches your public-facing domain. 3) For cloud n8n: ensure the webhook is in 'Production' mode (not 'Test'). 4) Check firewall/security groups allow inbound traffic on the webhook port.
Can multiple AI agents share the same n8n instance?
Yes. Each workflow can have its own webhook URL. Different AI agents (or the same agent with different configs) can trigger different workflows independently.
